titleStmt.title: Transcription and Translation of BL Cotton Ch. xv.21

respStmt.resp: Exported from Models of Authority data
respStmt.name: tei_exporter

availability.p: Status unknown. Exported for research and migration workflows.

revisionDesc.change: transcription moa=680 status=live modified=2021-04-05 08:50:42.252786+00 | translation moa=681 status=live modified=2021-04-05 08:50:44.667952+00

div.p:
M Rex Scotie Episcopis . Abbatibus . Iusticiis . Comitibus . Uicecomitibus . Baronibus . Ministris .
et omnibus probis hominibus totius regni sui et Honoris de Huntedune Clericis et laicis .
Salutem . Sciant tam posteri quam presentes me dedisse et concessisse . et per hanc meam cartam confir
masse Deo et Sancte MARIE et monachis ordinis Cistercii qui sunt de domo de Sartis . inperpetuam
elemosinam ad abbatiam construendam Pro salute anime mee et antecessorum et successorum
meorum totam Saltreiam cum omnibus pertinentiis suis in bosco et in Plano . in Pratis et aquis .
et Pasturis et Mariscis . et omnibus aliis rebus sicut eam tenuit bone memorie dauid rex a
uus meus . et sicut iam michi reddidit Henricus rex Anglie cum aliis rectis meis de Hunte
dune . Quare uolo et firmiter precipio ut predicti monachi eam habeant et teneant
de me et de heredibus meis sicut meam propriam elemosinam bene et in pace et honori
fice et quiete . et libere ab omni exactione temporali et seruitio sicut alii monachi eiusdem
ordinis in regno meo elemosinas suas liberius et quietius habent et tenent . HIS
testibus Herberto episcopo Glasc’ . Waldeuo abbate de Malros . Ernaldo abbate de Ruch’b’.
Osberto abbate de Ieddewrd’ . Waltero cancellario . Adam capellano de Roch’b’ . Nicholao
clerico . Willelmo fratre meo . Waltero filio Alani . Ricardo de Moreuilla . Rand’ . de
Sulis . Willelmo de Moreuilla . Willelmo de Sumeruilla . Liolfo filio Maccus . Or
mo filio Eilaui . DauiD olifard . Roberto Frebern . Dauid de Boeuilla . H . de perci .
Apud Rochesburd’;

Malcolm, king of Scotland, to bishops, abbots, justices, earls, sheriffs, barons, officials, and all worthy men of his entire kingdom and of the Honour of Huntingdon, clerk and lay, greeting. Let those to come after as well as those present know me to have given, conceded, and by this my charter to have made firm, to God, Saint Mary, and the monks of the Cistercian Order who are at the house of Sartis, in everlasting alms for the construction of an abbey, for the salvation of my own soul and my predecessors' and successors' [souls], the whole of Sawtry, with all its pertinents in wood and plain, in meadows and waters, and pastures and marshes, and all other property, just as David the king, my grandfather of good memory, held it, and just as Henry, king of England, has now returned to me with my other rights of Huntingdon. Wherefore I will and firmly command that the aforesaid monks have and hold it of me and my heirs, just as my own alms; well, in peace, honourably, quitly, and free from all temporal exaction and service, just as other monks of the same order in my kingdom most freely and quitly have and hold their alms. With these as witnesses: Herbert, bishop of Glasgow; Waldef, abbot of Melrose; Arnold, abbot of Roxburgh; Osbert, abbot of Jedburgh; Walter the chancellor; Adam the chaplain of Roxburgh; Nicholas the clerk; William my brother; Walter fitz Alan; Richard de Morville; Ranulf de Soulis; William de Morville; William de Somerville; Liulf son of Maccus; Orm son of Eilaf; David Oliphant; Robert Frebern; David de Boeville; Henry de Percy. At Roxburgh.
